The steelmaker serves the automotive, construction, appliance, energy, containers and packaging industries. U.S. Steel has an annual raw steelmaking capability of 22.4 million net tons. Barclays Capital Inc., Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC and Evercore Inc. are serving as financial advisers to U. S. Steel. Nippon Steel secured the deal to acquire U.S. Steel last December, outbidding several rivals including Cleveland-Cliffs, ArcelorMittal and Nucor. (Justin Merriman/Bloomberg via Getty Images / Getty Images)

Nippon Steel, which is based in Japan but is not a state-owned enterprise, has worked with U.S. Steel in an effort to assuage the concerns about the merger raised by politicians and labor groups.", "score": 0.62495816, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://finance.yahoo.com/news/us-steel-nippon-steel-merger-102356390.html", "title": "US Steel and Nippon Steel merger gains non-US regulator approval", "content": "US Steel president and CEO David Burritt said: "We are pleased with the regulatory approvals received, as they are a clear indication that the transaction with Nippon Steel is pro-competitive and supports the strategic merits of foreign investment.

And, under Cliffs, all the white-collar staff is almost certainly gone.", "score": 0.5518883, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://gmk.center/en/news/biden-plans-to-block-the-merger-of-nippon-steel-and-us-steel-bloomberg", "title": "Biden plans to block the merger of Nippon Steel and US Steel - Bloomberg", "content": "In early December, Nippon Steel reaffirmed its confidence that the acquisition of US Steel could be completed by the end of 2024 if the deal goes through the proper procedures and is approved by the Biden administration.
